project.name: Sidbury 187
project.approvingAuthority: New Hanover County Technical Review Committee
project.summary: Sidbury 187 is a large residential subdivision planned for northern New Hanover County near Castle Hayne. WilmingtonBiz reported that the project would be built on just over 700 acres bounded by Interstate 140, Sidbury Road, and Crooked Pine Road, adjacent to Sidbury Station. The plan includes 1,751 total housing units across single-family homes, townhomes, and duplexes, with D.R. Horton identified as the developer/builder and McKim & Creed tied to the project plans. The article also reported a three-phase buildout, with full buildout expected in 2032.
project.locationDescription: Northern New Hanover County near Castle Hayne, on land bounded by Interstate 140, Sidbury Road, and Crooked Pine Road, next to Sidbury Station.
project.owner: Corbett Package Co. and Corbett Industries
project.latestUpdate: WilmingtonBiz reported that the project went before New Hanover County's Technical Review Committee in June 2024.
project.nextStep: Verify the official New Hanover County TRC packet, approval letter, and final construction-plan status before public publication.

traffic.trafficCounts: 14,093 daily vehicle trips; 999 AM peak-hour trips; 1,378 PM peak-hour trips.
traffic.roadImprovements: The article reports the traffic impact analysis recommended extending existing turn lanes, adding new turn lanes, and installing new traffic lights.
traffic.notes: Traffic totals and improvements are from WilmingtonBiz's summary of the traffic impact analysis. Replace with the official TIA PDF if the county document is located.

reviewNotes: This is an interim package generated from the WilmingtonBiz article and New Hanover County's public residential project listing.
Find and replace with the official New Hanover County TRC packet, site plan, and traffic impact analysis when available.
Use 700 acres only as a sortable numeric approximation; public copy should say just over 700 acres.
No formal case number or parcel IDs were verified from the article.
The project name appears in county records as Sidbury 187; the article describes the land as Sidbury 187 and Gore tracts.
